name: Calzone

ingredients.item: 1/4 cups 00 flour plus semolina flour for dusting
slices of prosciutto, you can also use ham

method: In a large bowl, add flour and salt. Proof the yeast in 1 cup of warm water. Let rest until bubbles form on top, about 10 minutes. Add yeast water to flour mixture slowly, mixing until you have formed a wet dough. Add a little more flour until you can handle the dough without sticking and knead for 5 minutes. Form a ball, place it in a bowl with a dish towel on top, and let rise for about an hour, or until doubled. Punch down and let rise a second time for an hour. After an hour, or when the dough is doubled in size, punch down and transfer dough to a semolina-dusted cutting board. Preheat oven to 450°F. With a rolling pin, roll out to12-inches round. Cut in half. In the middle of each half put 3-4 T of the cheese mixture. Season with salt & pepper. Lay prosciutto (or ham) on top and fold over. With wet fingers, pinch together dough to close, see picture. On a parchment-lined baking sheet place your calzones, make a small slit on top to vent, bake calzone on bottom rack until browned on bottom. Remove from oven. Place rack on top and broil (500°) until brown and bubbly. Makes 2 calzones. Olé
